label: Wife · 研發的老婆大人
prompt: A horizontal 4:3 sepia-toned plein-air sketch in the style of an old apothecary diary illustration — single warm earth-tone (muted gold #C8A24A) on textured cream paper, light cross-hatching, sumi-e brush study crossed with mid-century linocut feel. The scene depicts a quiet kitchen workshop counter from a slightly elevated three-quarter angle: a small copper soap-making pot sits at the centre on a wooden trivet with a long wooden stir spoon resting against its rim, a cedar curing rack stands behind it holding several finished rectangular soap bars in different stages of drying, an open formula notebook lies in the foreground with handwriting flowing across the page (writing suggested by abstract brush strokes, no readable characters of any script), small clear glass jars of dried botanicals (a sprig of dried mugwort, calendula petals, lavender stems) clustered to one side, and a small ceramic bowl of poured oil. Soft natural daylight comes from an unseen window to the upper left casting gentle shadows. No face, no full figure of a person, no hands, no text, no signatures, no logos, no border. Quiet, devoted, contemplative — the workshop of a careful formulator who insists on what her own grandmother could use.

label: Husband · 寫字的創作老公
prompt: A horizontal 4:3 sepia-toned plein-air sketch in matching style — single warm earth-tone (muted gold #C8A24A) on textured cream paper, light cross-hatching, sumi-e brush study crossed with mid-century linocut feel. The scene depicts a runner-writer's quiet corner at dawn from a slightly elevated three-quarter angle: an aged wooden desk at the centre, an open notebook spread across the desk with handwritten lines flowing across two pages (writing suggested by abstract brush strokes, no readable characters of any script), a slim fountain pen lying diagonally across one page, a small pair of well-worn running shoes neatly placed on the floor at the foot of the desk, a stainless water bottle and a simple running watch resting on the desk corner, a small stack of three well-thumbed paperback books, a stoneware coffee cup with two thin lines of steam rising, and a window suggested in the background with morning light filtering through. No face, no full figure of a person, no hands, no text, no signatures, no logos, no border. Reflective, athletic, contemplative — the desk of a storyteller who runs at dawn before the keyboard.
